subject: Integrate CryptoUtils into common
commit_message:
Integrate CryptoUtils into common

Create PropertyCoder and update PropertyUtil for enhancement

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

A few general comments:

1.  No changes should be necessary under "common-logging" flexlogger package.      I doubt there are any uses in any repos for encrypted data that relates to flexlogger.   FlexLogger is not recommended in any case.   This file seems like a plain copy of the one in drools-pdp repo.   Therefore let's leave this functionality untouched here, so all that code I think should go away.   Eventually should get rid of that class.

2.  I believe we should not use environment variables in a generic library, commons is for generic libraries, as this goes beyond imposing constraints in java code, it imposes constraints to the installer, and the OS where this library will be run.

3.  I started to put together some code in an in-progress review, see:  https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/policy/drools-pdp/+/97229/.  I
ll be adding you and Jim while in progress to discuss.  Note that there the code is very simple and will replace all the code under flexlogger package for the drools-pdp.   I will add next getting the secret key in the drools pdp, so you have it as a reference.   In the future, we may want to move all that to commons but is OK for now.   We can discuss engine in a later iteration.

Patch Set 4:

> A few general comments:
 > 
 > 1.  No changes should be necessary under "common-logging"
 > flexlogger package.      I doubt there are any uses in any repos
 > for encrypted data that relates to flexlogger.   FlexLogger is not
 > recommended in any case.   This file seems like a plain copy of the
 > one in drools-pdp repo.   Therefore let's leave this functionality
 > untouched here, so all that code I think should go away.  
 > Eventually should get rid of that class.
 > 
 > 2.  I believe we should not use environment variables in a generic
 > library, commons is for generic libraries, as this goes beyond
 > imposing constraints in java code, it imposes constraints to the
 > installer, and the OS where this library will be run.
 > 
 > 3.  I started to put together some code in an in-progress review,
 > see:  https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/policy/drools-pdp/+/97229/.  I
 > ll be adding you and Jim while in progress to discuss.  Note that
 > there the code is very simple and will replace all the code under
 > flexlogger package for the drools-pdp.   I will add next getting
 > the secret key in the drools pdp, so you have it as a reference.  
 > In the future, we may want to move all that to commons but is OK
 > for now.   We can discuss engine in a later iteration.

regarding #1: can we verify that no encrypted properties are used by flexlogger internally?  If that's the case, then no changes are needed in common-logging.  If encrypted properties ARE used by flexlogger internally, then this would still be needed.
regarding #2: so then expected the invoker to pass the cryto (possibly null) to PropertyCoder rather than constructing one.
regarding #3: since your version doesn't care about "enc:" properties when crypto==null, is it OK to avoid a similar check/warning in PropertyCoder?

 > regarding #2: so then expected the invoker to pass the cryto
 > (possibly null) to PropertyCoder rather than constructing one.
 > regarding #3: since your version doesn't care about "enc:"
 > properties when crypto==null, is it OK to avoid a similar
 > check/warning in PropertyCoder?

#1 - per Joseph not needed, in any case, flexlogger if only used by PE, may be should be moved all together there.
#2 - right, the though is that each "concrete application" will use the method they find more suitable to retrieve the key, this is to avoid imposing installation and OS constraints into the generic library.
#3 - probably good to warn, I'll have to add it to my version.

Patch Set 4:

1) so lets remove the changes to common-logging
2) turns out we can't pass a crypto util to PropertyCoder; it must either get it from the environment or from the json file, itself.  Sounds like you do not want it to be gotten from the environment, so it will always have to get it from the json file.  The one thing we could do, however, is pass in the name of the json field that contains the key, rather than assuming it's "AES_ENCRYPTION_KEY".
Abandoned
